title: The Analysis of Mind
description: The Analysis of Mind — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "心的分析" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: Abstraction
memes.description: 'Russell''s behaviorist account of abstraction — the cognitive process by which general ideas are formed from particulars, explained through mnemic physiology rather than mental acts.'

memes.label: Act-Content-Object
memes.description: 'Franz Brentano''s tripartite framework distinguishing the mental act from its content and its object, foundational to modern theories of intentionality'

memes.label: Alexius Meinong
memes.description: 'Austrian philosopher, student of Brentano, known for his theory of objects (Gegenstandstheorie) and the act-content-object distinction in thought'

memes.label: Analysis
memes.description: 'Russell''s method of philosophical analysis as applied to mental phenomena — decomposing complex psychological concepts into simpler, epistemologically primitive constituents through logical clarification.'

memes.label: Animal
memes.description: 'The category of living organisms whose behaviour Russell treats as a primary source of evidence for psychological theory — animal psychology provides the comparative method that challenges anthropocentric assumptions about mind and consciousness'

memes.label: Appearance
memes.description: In Russell's analysis, the sensory presentation of an object from a particular perspective; appearances form the system of data from which physical objects are constructed as logical fictions

memes.label: Association
memes.description: 'The psychological law whereby mental events become linked through contiguity or similarity, contrasted by Russell with physical causation.'

memes.label: Attention
memes.description: 'The selective focus of consciousness or behavior upon a subset of available stimuli — a psychological concept that Russell employs to analyse how meaning is narrowed and how introspection functions as a method'

memes.label: Awareness
memes.description: 'A non-Cartesian conception of consciousness as a relational property — awareness *of* something — rather than a substantial mental state; central to Russell''s critique of traditional consciousness and his turn toward neutral monism.'

memes.label: Behaviour
memes.description: 'Observable bodily activity treated by Russell and the behaviourist school as the primary datum for psychological explanation, bypassing introspection'

memes.label: Behaviourism
memes.description: 'A psychological school founded by John B. Watson that restricts scientific psychology to externally observable behaviour, denying introspection as a valid method'

memes.label: Belief
memes.description: 'The central problem in the analysis of mind — a present occurrence with propositional content that stands in a truth-or-falsehood relation to an objective fact'

memes.label: Bertrand Russell
memes.description: "British philosopher (1872–1970), logician, and author of The Analysis of Mind, in which he develops a neutral monist account of mental phenomena synthesizing behaviourist psychology with philosophical analysis"

memes.label: Body
memes.description: 'The physical organism whose movements and habits are central to Russell''s behaviourist analysis of mind; the body is the observable locus where mnemic phenomena, habit, and voluntary action intersect with psychological explanation.'

memes.label: Brain
memes.description: 'The physical organ whose modifications Russell identifies as the physiological substrate of memory, habit, and mental disorder — central to his argument that mental phenomena are causally dependent on material processes'

memes.label: 'Causal Laws'
memes.description: "Russell's analysis of the distinction between psychological and physical causal laws, and his critique of traditional causation in favour of laws of change, developed in Lecture V of The Analysis of Mind"

memes.label: Cause
memes.description: 'The relation between events where one brings about another — central to Russell''s analysis of mnemic causation and the scientific study of mind'

memes.label: Certainty
memes.description: 'The felt degree of conviction accompanying belief — analyzed by Russell as a product of habit rather than a reliable indicator of truth, and distinguished from the logical grounds that justify belief'

memes.label: Cognition
memes.description: 'The broad family of mental processes involving knowledge, recognition, and judgment — analyzed by Russell as natural phenomena rather than manifestations of a substantial cognitive subject, encompassing both propositional knowledge and behavioral recognition.'

memes.label: Consciousness
memes.description: 'The popularly supposed essence of mental phenomena, which Russell argues is not an entity but a composite construction'

memes.label: Content (Mental)
memes.description: 'The intermediate term in the Brentano-Meinong threefold act-content-object analysis of thought — what distinguishes thoughts that refer to the same object in different ways'

memes.label: "Contents"
memes.description: "Contents"

memes.label: David Hume
memes.description: "Scottish empiricist philosopher (1711–1776) whose distinction between impressions and ideas, and whose sceptical analysis of the self, provide Russell with essential conceptual tools in his analysis of sensation, images, and introspection"

memes.label: Definition
memes.description: 'The specification of the meaning of a term — a methodological practice that Russell employs extensively throughout The Analysis of Mind to clarify concepts, demarcate scientific domains, and expose the confusions embedded in ordinary language'

memes.label: Desire
memes.description: 'A mental or behavioural phenomenon analysed by Russell as a causal law of behaviour rather than an irreducible mental state; desire is treated as functionally analogous to force in mechanics'

memes.label: 'Desire and Feeling'
memes.description: 'Russell''s behaviouristic analysis of desire as a causal law of behaviour, and its relation to feeling-tone'

memes.label: Disposition
memes.description: 'Russell''s concept of disposition as a latent capacity — central to his behaviorist accounts of belief, memory, and desire, contrasting active occurrence with permanent tendency.'

memes.label: Doubt
memes.description: 'A mental attitude of suspended judgment or uncertainty regarding a proposition, distinguished from both belief and disbelief in the analysis of mind'

memes.label: Dream
memes.description: 'Dreams in Russell''s Analysis of Mind — as a special class of images occurring during sleep, distinct from sensations yet feeling just as real, and functioning through mnemic causation rather than physical laws'

memes.label: Dualism
memes.description: 'The philosophical view that mind and matter are two fundamentally distinct kinds of substance — examined and rejected by Russell in favor of neutral monism'

memes.label: Emotion
memes.description: 'A mental phenomenon analysed by Russell as causally linked to sensations, images, and bodily states, distinct from purely cognitive states such as belief.'

memes.label: 'Emotions and Will'
memes.description: 'Russell''s analysis of emotions as complex physiological processes, and will as a form of desire-driven behaviour cycle'

memes.label: Ernst Mach
memes.description: "Austrian physicist and philosopher (1838–1916) whose analysis of sensations as the common ground of mental and physical is acknowledged by Russell in The Analysis of Mind as a precursor to his own neutral monism"

memes.label: Error
memes.description: 'Error in Russell''s Analysis of Mind — the possibility of false belief and mistaken judgment, which serves as evidence of the sophistication of cognition and is central to understanding knowledge, truth, and belief'

memes.label: Evidence
memes.description: The grounds for belief — in Russell's analysis, either the testimony of sense-experience or the logical criterion of self-evidence and coherence that distinguishes knowledge from mere true belief

memes.label: Existence
memes.description: In Brentano's psychology adopted by Russell, the "intentional inexistence" of an object within a mental act — the distinctive way in which psychological phenomena contain their objects, as opposed to the independent existence attributed to physical things

memes.label: Expectation
memes.description: 'A form of belief directed toward the future, characterised by images of anticipated sensations accompanied by a belief-feeling, and distinguished by Russell from memory (past-directed) and bare assent (non-temporal) as one of three fundamental kinds of belief.'

memes.label: Experience
memes.description: 'The neutral stuff of which the world is composed in Russell''s monistic framework — borrowed from William James, experience is neither inherently mental nor physical but can be taken in either context'

memes.label: Fact
memes.description: 'The observable occurrences that serve as the starting point for scientific and philosophical inquiry, which Russell treats as neutral particulars — neither mental nor physical — from which knowledge is constructed'

memes.label: Feeling
memes.description: 'The affective component of mental life — one of three fundamental classifications of mental phenomena in Russell''s analysis, alongside knowledge and desire'

memes.label: Franz Brentano
memes.description: 'Austrian philosopher-psychologist whose intentionality thesis deeply influenced Russell, Husserl, and the phenomenological tradition'

memes.label: 'General Ideas and Thought'
memes.description: "Russell's analysis of general ideas in Lecture XI, arguing that abstract thought does not require abstract mental contents, only particular images functioning as signs for classes of particulars"

memes.label: Habit
memes.description: 'Acquired patterns of behaviour and thought that Russell analyses as central to the constitution of mind, distinguishing habit from instinct and linking it to knowledge and language.'

memes.label: Henri Bergson
memes.description: "French philosopher (1859–1941) whose theories of duration, intuition, and the elan vital are critically engaged by Russell in The Analysis of Mind, particularly regarding instinct and voluntary action"

memes.label: Idealism
memes.description: 'The philosophical view that reality is fundamentally mental — critiqued by Russell in The Analysis of Mind as making consciousness into a "fetish"'

memes.label: Images
memes.description: 'Mental contents that are "centrally excited" rather than caused by immediate sensory stimulation, distinguished from sensations by their causal origin rather than by intrinsic qualities, and central to Russell''s neutral monist account of mind.'

memes.label: Imagination
memes.description: 'The mental capacity to form images not directly tied to past sensory experience or present perception, distinguished by Russell from memory primarily by accompanying beliefs and feeling-tone rather than by intrinsic image content.'

memes.label: Immanuel Kant
memes.description: "German philosopher (1724–1804) whose tripartite division of mental phenomena and theory of knowledge are referenced by Russell in The Analysis of Mind as landmarks in the philosophical tradition he critically reexamines"

memes.label: Inference
memes.description: 'The process by which beliefs are derived from other beliefs or from sensory data, which Russell treats as a fallible empirical phenomenon rather than a transparent rational faculty'

memes.label: Instinct
memes.description: 'Innate, unlearned patterns of movement and behaviour that Russell contrasts with habit, analysing the gradual maturation and adaptive limits of instinct in both animals and humans.'

memes.label: 'Instinct and Habit'
memes.description: 'Russell''s behaviouristic distinction between innate instinctive movements and acquired habits as the two fundamental sources of action'

memes.label: Intentionality
memes.description: 'The characteristic of mental phenomena whereby they are "about" or "directed toward" an object, a concept central to Brentano''s psychology and contested by Russell in The Analysis of Mind.'

memes.label: 'Introspection'
memes.description: 'Russell''s critique of introspection as a privileged source of self-knowledge, and his reconception of it as a form of ordinary observation'

memes.label: John B. Watson
memes.description: 'American psychologist (1878–1958), founder of behaviourism, whose externalist methodology Russell draws on in his analysis of mind'

memes.label: John Locke
memes.description: "English philosopher (1632–1704) whose empiricist theory of ideas is referenced by Russell in The Analysis of Mind as part of the British philosophical tradition that shaped discussions of memory, images, and abstract thought"

memes.label: Judgment
memes.description: 'A mental act of affirming or denying a proposition; in Russell''s analysis, judgment is closely tied to belief, recognition, and the formation of general ideas from vague images.'

memes.label: Knight Dunlap
memes.description: "American psychologist (1875–1949) and critic of introspection methodology, whose article 'The Case against Introspection' is examined in detail by Russell in his analysis of psychological method"

memes.label: Knowledge
memes.description: 'Knowledge in Russell''s Analysis of Mind — a behaviourist and neutral monist account of knowledge as a form of sensitivity constituted by habits, not a mysterious subject-object relation'

memes.label: Language
memes.description: 'A bodily habit analysed by Russell in behaviourist terms as the acquired association between words and their effects, serving practical communication and thought without requiring a mental act of "meaning"'

memes.label: Learning
memes.description: 'Learning in Russell''s Analysis of Mind — the process by which behaviour is modified through experience, distinguished from instinct and explained through the laws of mnemic causation and habit formation'

memes.label: "Lecture I: Recent Criticisms of 'Consciousness'"
memes.description: "Lecture I: Recent Criticisms of 'Consciousness'"

memes.label: "Lecture II: Instinct and Habit"

memes.description: "Lecture IV: Influence of Past History on Present Occurrences in Living Beings"

memes.description: "Lecture IX: Memory"

memes.label: "Lecture V: Psychological and Physical Causal Laws"
memes.description: "Lecture V: Psychological and Physical Causal Laws"

memes.description: "Lecture VI: Introspection"

memes.label: "Lecture VII: The Definition of Perception"
memes.description: "Lecture VII: The Definition of Perception"

memes.description: "Lecture VIII: Sensations and Images"

memes.description: "Lecture X: Words and Meaning"

memes.label: "Lecture XI: General Ideas and Thought"
memes.description: "Lecture XI: General Ideas and Thought"

memes.label: "Lecture XII: Belief"
memes.description: "Lecture XII: Belief"

memes.description: "Lecture XIII: Truth and Falsehood"

memes.label: "Lecture XIV: Emotions and Will"
memes.description: "Lecture XIV: Emotions and Will"

memes.label: "Lecture XV: Characteristics of Mental Phenomena"
memes.description: "Lecture XV: Characteristics of Mental Phenomena"

memes.label: Logic
memes.description: 'Russell''s logical-analytic method as the foundation for philosophical analysis of mind, emphasizing logical constructions and the primacy of logical form in philosophical inquiry.'

memes.label: Materialism
memes.description: "In Russell's analysis, the doctrine that matter is the fundamental reality and mind a property of physical substance; also, the methodological view that mental phenomena are causally dependent upon physical phenomena"

memes.label: Matter
memes.description: 'The substance of the physical world as traditionally conceived; Russell argues that matter is not the ultimate stuff of reality but a logical construction from events, and that it is neither more solid nor more fundamental than mind.'

memes.label: Meaning
memes.description: 'Russell''s behaviourist account of linguistic meaning, according to which word meaning is constituted by habits of use rather than by mental acts of understanding'

memes.label: Memory
memes.description: 'A form of knowledge involving images accompanied by feelings of familiarity and pastness, analysed by Russell without appeal to a knowing subject'

memes.label: Memory-Image
memes.description: "An image accompanied by a feeling of pastness and a memory-belief, distinguished by Russell from both sensation and imagination-images through its characteristic feeling of familiarity and reference to past experience"

memes.label: 'Mental Phenomena'
memes.description: "Russell's analysis in Lecture XV of what distinguishes mental phenomena from physical phenomena, concluding that mind and matter are distinguished by their causal laws rather than by intrinsic qualities"

memes.label: Mind
memes.description: "The analysis of mind as a logical construction from sensations, images, and their relations, rejecting the notion of mind as a unitary substance or subject."

memes.label: Mnemic Causation
memes.description: 'A form of causation in which the proximate cause consists of past occurrences rather than present stimuli, introduced by Russell to explain memory, habit, and learned behaviour without positing mental substances'

memes.label: 'Mnemic Phenomena'
memes.description: "Russell's account, following Richard Semon, of mnemic phenomena — responses of organisms that depend essentially on past history, forming the basis of psychological causal laws"

memes.label: Nervous System
memes.description: 'The physiological substrate of habit, memory, and mental phenomena in Russell''s behaviorist analysis of mind.'

memes.label: Neutral Monism
memes.description: 'The metaphysical doctrine, endorsed by Russell, that mind and matter are composed of a single "neutral stuff" which is intrinsically neither mental nor material.'

memes.label: Object
memes.description: 'The referent or target of a mental act — in Brentano and Meinong''s terminology, that toward which thought is directed, and which Russell ultimately argues is not essentially mental'

memes.label: Observation
memes.description: 'The empirical method of gathering data through the senses — which Russell treats as the foundation of scientific psychology and the corrective to the fallibility of introspection, arguing that only what is open to external observation can serve as reliable evidence for a theory of mind'

memes.label: Pain
memes.description: 'A distinct sensory modality carefully distinguished by Russell from the affective property of discomfort, with which pain has been systematically confused in philosophical accounts of mind'

memes.label: Particulars
memes.description: "The concrete sensory elements (sense-data) that constitute physical objects through systematic correlation across different perspectives, in the Russellian theory of perception."

memes.label: Percept
memes.description: In Russell's analysis, the object of a perceptual act — the sensory presentation that perception delivers, distinct from the physiological process of perceiving and from the inferred physical object

memes.label: Perception
memes.description: 'The actual experience that combines a core of pure sensation with mnemic additions from past experience, analysed by Russell in physical terms without appeal to a knowing subject'

memes.label: Perspective
memes.description: In Russell's neutral monism, the set of all appearances or particulars available at a given place at a given time; perspectives are the fundamental units of perceptual experience from which both physical objects and biographical minds are constructed as logical fictions

memes.label: Physics
memes.description: 'The science of matter and its motions — which Russell uses as a methodological foil to define the distinctiveness of psychology, while ultimately arguing that the laws of physics and psychology converge in a neutral monist framework'

memes.label: Physiology
memes.description: 'The biological science of bodily function that Russell positions as the methodological foundation for a scientific psychology.'

memes.label: Pleasure
memes.description: 'The affective property marking the successful completion of a behaviour-cycle, defined by Russell through its causal role rather than intrinsic quality'

memes.label: "Preface"
memes.description: "Preface"

memes.label: Presentation
memes.description: In Russell's analysis, a mode of consciousness in which something is presented to the mind without affirmation or denial; one term in Brentano's tripartite classification of mental phenomena alongside judgment and feeling

memes.label: Privacy
memes.description: 'The property by which mental occurrences are accessible to only one observer — analyzed by Russell as a contingent physiological limitation rather than a metaphysical mark of the mental, and therefore no bar to scientific psychology'

memes.label: Proposition
memes.description: 'The content of a belief, expressible in words or images, that can be asserted or denied; Russell extends the term beyond linguistic entities to include image-based contents that may ante-date language.'

memes.label: Psychoanalysis
memes.description: 'The psychological school founded by Sigmund Freud, whose concept of unconscious desires Russell uses to argue that consciousness is not essential to mind'

memes.label: Psychology
memes.description: 'The science of mental phenomena, which Russell approaches from a behaviourist and neutral-monist standpoint, treating its subject matter as continuous with physics rather than as a separate realm of private experience'

memes.label: Pure Experience
memes.description: "A term adopted from William James designating the primordial stuff of reality — prior to the distinction between subject and object, mind and matter — central to neutral monism."

memes.label: Realism
memes.description: 'The philosophical position that the objects of perception and thought exist independently of the mind, defended by Russell in qualified form and distinguished from idealism by its insistence on the mind-independent status of the data of sensation and knowledge.'

memes.label: Reality
memes.description: 'Russell''s realist conception of reality — the external world as independent of mind yet accessible through sensation, distinguished from the merely psychological feeling of reality that accompanies certain mental states.'

memes.label: Reason
memes.description: 'The cognitive faculty or process of drawing inferences — which Russell treats not as an a priori mental power but as a habit-based capacity grounded in experience, association, and the causal structure of the world'

memes.label: Recognition
memes.description: In Russell's analysis, a form of memory-knowledge involving the judgment that a present sensation belongs to a familiar kind or has been experienced before; distinct from both habit-memory and independent recollection

memes.label: Recollection
memes.description: In Russell's analysis, the conscious recall of a specific past event with its temporal context; distinguished from habit-memory by its reference to a definite past occurrence and from mere recognition by its richer temporal and contextual content

memes.label: Reference
memes.description: In Russell's analysis, the directedness of mental content toward an object; derived from Brentano's thesis that mental phenomena have essential reference to objects, which Russell critically examines and partially rejects

memes.label: Reflex
memes.description: 'Reflex action in Russell''s Analysis of Mind — the simplest class of movement, distinguished from voluntary action and mechanical movement, and serving as a building block for understanding the hierarchy of behaviour from reflex to instinct to habit'

memes.label: Relation
memes.description: 'A fundamental category of ontology that Russell places at the center of his philosophical method — entities are constituted not by intrinsic essences but by their relations to other entities, a view that underpins both his neutral monism and his rejection of the subject-object duality'

memes.label: René Descartes
memes.description: "French philosopher (1596–1650) whose substance dualism and mind-body interaction problem are critically discussed by Russell in The Analysis of Mind as the historical origin of the untenable separation between mental and physical"

memes.label: Richard Semon
memes.description: "German zoologist and evolutionary biologist (1859–1918) whose theory of mnemic phenomena and the engram provided Russell with the foundational framework for his analysis of memory and habit in living organisms"

memes.label: Science
memes.description: 'The systematic study of observable regularities, which Russell treats as the model for a properly naturalistic psychology — one that builds from publicly observable data rather than private introspection'

memes.label: Self
memes.description: 'The supposed subject of experience that Russell dissolves into a logical construction, arguing that the self is not a datum of immediate experience but a convenient fiction built up from particular mental occurrences'

memes.label: Sensation
memes.description: 'Raw sensory input — the fundamental building block of experience in Russell''s neutral monism, distinct from images and directly known through perception'

memes.label: 'Sensation and Images'
memes.description: 'The fundamental distinction between sensations (non-mnemic, publicly accessible) and images (mnemic, private) in Russell''s neutral monism'

memes.label: Sense-Data
memes.description: 'The immediate objects of sensation that Russell distinguishes from the physical stimulus and from the conscious act of sensing, a distinction central to his analysis of perception.'

memes.label: Sigmund Freud
memes.description: "Austrian neurologist and founder of psychoanalysis (1856–1939), whose theory of the unconscious and the mechanism of repression is critically examined by Russell in his analysis of desire and consciousness"

memes.label: Sign
memes.description: 'A sensible particular whose function is to indicate or stand for something other than itself — a central concept in the analysis of language, meaning, and mental content'

memes.label: Stimulus and Response
memes.description: 'The behaviorist framework for analysing action whereby environmental stimuli elicit organismic responses, adopted by Russell as a methodological basis for understanding mental phenomena without appealing to introspective data or non-physical causes.'

memes.label: Subject–Object
memes.description: 'The traditional philosophical dualism between the knowing subject and the known object, which Russell critically examines and ultimately dissolves through his neutral monist analysis of mind.'

memes.label: Subjectivity
memes.description: In Russell's analysis, the perspectival character of experience defined by the grouping of appearances around a passive place; subjectivity is not a mental substance but a structural feature of how sensory data are organized

memes.label: Tendency
memes.description: In Russell's analysis, a dispositional property invoked in behaviourist explanations of desire, habit, and volition; a statistical regularity in behaviour rather than a mental entity, representing the probability that an organism will act in a certain way under given conditions

memes.label: Thought
memes.description: 'A mental occurrence whose structure Russell analyzes in terms of act, content, and object — ultimately arguing that thought requires no non-physical substance and that its apparent object-directedness is derivative, not essential'

memes.label: Time
memes.description: 'Time as analysed in Russell''s Analysis of Mind — as a feature of conscious experience rather than a metaphysical absolute, with past and future distinguished through memory and expectation'

memes.label: Truth
memes.description: 'Truth in Russell''s Analysis of Mind — a behaviouristic correspondence theory: a belief is true when the events that would verify it actually occur, and truth consists in the causal relation between belief and fact'

memes.label: 'Truth and Falsehood'
memes.description: "Russell's behaviouristic and relational account of truth and falsehood as correspondence between belief and fact, developed in Lecture XIII of The Analysis of Mind"

memes.label: Unconscious
memes.description: 'Mental states — desires, beliefs, and wishes — that influence behaviour without being present to consciousness, which Russell reinterprets in causal terms rather than as contents of a hidden mental realm'

memes.label: Universals
memes.description: 'General concepts or properties shared across multiple particulars — analyzed by Russell through the psychological process of abstraction and the use of general signs, rather than as independently existing Platonic entities'

memes.label: Verification
memes.description: "The process by which a belief or expectation is confirmed through subsequent sensation, analysed by Russell as the fundamental criterion of truth without requiring correspondence to an inaccessible reality"

memes.label: Verification Conditions
memes.description: In Russell's analysis, the conditions under which a belief or proposition is confirmed by experience; verification consists ultimately in the happening of something expected, forming the bridge between belief and knowledge

memes.label: Volition
memes.description: 'The mental act of willing or choosing — a concept Russell treats sceptically, arguing that what we call "volitions" are better understood as causal sequences of desire and habit rather than as irreducible acts of a free will'

memes.label: Voluntary Movement
memes.description: "A class of bodily movements analysed by Russell as distinguished from reflex and mechanical movements by the involvement of an idea of the end to be achieved, forming a key link between desire, images, and action in his behaviourist framework"

memes.label: William James
memes.description: 'American philosopher and psychologist (1842–1910), founder of pragmatism and radical empiricism, whose rejection of consciousness as an entity deeply influenced Russell''s neutral monism'

memes.label: Wish
memes.description: 'In Russell''s analysis, the psychological phenomenon of wish — both conscious and unconscious — examined through Freudian theory and behaviorist critique.'

memes.label: 'Words and Meaning'
memes.description: 'Russell''s behaviouristic account of linguistic meaning as a causal law connecting words to their referents and effects'
